entries.name: Age
entries: Aasimar mature at the same rate as humans, but they can live up to 160 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Aasimar have the same range of height and weight as humans.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Blessed with a radiant soul, your vision can easily cut through darkness. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Celestial Resistance
entries: You have resistance to necrotic damage and radiant damage.

entries.name: Healing Hands
entries: As an action, you can touch a creature and cause it to regain a number of hit points equal to your level. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Light Bearer
entries: You know the {@spell light} cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Celestial.

entries.name: Age
entries: Bugbears reach adulthood at age 16 and live up to 80 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Bugbears are between 6 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 250 and 350 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Long-Limbed
entries: When you make a melee attack on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Sneaky
entries: You are proficient in the {@skill Stealth} skill.

entries.name: Surprise Attack
entries: If you surprise a creature and hit it with an attack on your first turn in combat, the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage to it. You can use this trait only once per combat.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your lineage stems from a dragon progenitor. Choose the kind of dragon from the Draconic Ancestors table. Your choice affects your Breath Weapon and {@variantrule Damage} {@variantrule Resistance} traits as well as your appearance.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of magical energy in either a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|Cone} or a 30-foot {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|Line} that is 5 feet wide (choose the shape each time). Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d10} damage of the type determined by your Draconic Ancestry trait. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. This damage increases by {@damage 1d10} when you reach character levels 5 ({@damage 2d10}), 11 ({@damage 3d10}), and 17 ({@damage 4d10}).
You can use this Breath Weapon a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}.

entries.name: Damage Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance} to the damage type determined by your Draconic Ancestry trait.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Draconic Flight
entries: When you reach character level 5, you can channel draconic magic to give yourself temporary flight. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action}, you sprout spectral wings on your back that last for 10 minutes or until you retract the wings (no action required) or have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition. During that time, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed} equal to your {@variantrule Speed}. Your wings appear to be made of the same energy as your Breath Weapon. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-5 feet tall)

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Dwarven Resilience
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Poison damage. You also have {@variantrule Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned} condition.

entries.name: Dwarven Toughness
entries: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|Hit Point} maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level.

entries.name: Stonecunning
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action}, you gain {@sense Tremorsense} with a range of 60 feet for 10 minutes. You must be on a stone surface or touching a stone surface to use this {@sense Tremorsense}. The stone can be natural or worked.
You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 5-6 feet tall)

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Elven Lineage
entries: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a lineage from the Elven Lineages table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that lineage.

When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

entries.caption: Elven Lineages
rows: The range of your {@sense Darkvision} increases to 120 feet. You also know the {@spell Dancing Lights} cantrip.

You know the {@spell Prestidigitation} cantrip. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}, you can replace that cantrip with a different {@filter cantrip from the Wizard spell list|spells|level=0|class=Wizard}.
Your {@variantrule Speed} increases to 35 feet. You also know the {@spell Druidcraft} cantrip.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed} condition.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Insight}, {@skill Perception}, or {@skill Survival} skill.

entries.name: Trance
entries: You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a {@variantrule Long Rest} in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

items.name: Elven Lineage (Drow)
entries: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. The range of your {@sense Darkvision} increases to 120 feet. You also know the {@spell Dancing Lights} cantrip.

When you reach character level 3, you learn the {@spell Faerie Fire} spell. When you reach character level 5, you also learn the {@spell Darkness} spell. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each spell once without a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

items.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.

items.name: Elven Lineage (High Elf)
entries: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. You know the {@spell Prestidigitation} cantrip. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}, you can replace that cantrip with a different {@filter cantrip from the Wizard spell list|spells|level=0|class=Wizard}.

When you reach character level 3, you learn the {@spell Detect Magic} spell. When you reach character level 5, you also learn the {@spell Misty Step} spell. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each spell once without a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

items.name: Elven Lineage (Wood Elf)
entries: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Your {@variantrule Speed} increases to 35 feet. You also know the {@spell Druidcraft} cantrip.

When you reach character level 3, you learn the {@spell Longstrider} spell. When you reach character level 5, you also learn the {@spell Pass without Trace} spell. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each spell once without a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage).

entries.name: Age
entries: As humanoids related to the fey, firbolg have long lifespans. A firbolg reaches adulthood around 30, and the oldest of them can live for 500 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Firbolg are between 7 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 240 and 300 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Firbolg Magic
entries: You can cast {@spell detect magic} and {@spell disguise self} with this trait, using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability for them. Once you cast either spell, you can't cast it again with this trait until you finish a short or long rest. When you use this version of {@spell disguise self}, you can seem up to 3 feet shorter than normal, allowing you to more easily blend in with humans and elves.

entries.name: Hidden Step
entries: As a bonus action, you can magically turn {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn or until you attack, make a damage roll, or force someone to make a saving throw. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Speech of Beast and Leaf
entries: You have the ability to communicate in a limited manner with beasts and plants. They can understand the meaning of your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return. You have advantage on all Charisma checks you make to influence them.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common, Elvish, and Giant.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (about 3-4 feet tall)

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Gnomish Cunning
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

entries.name: Gnomish Lineage
entries: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose one of the following options; whichever one you choose, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the lineage):

items.name: Forest Gnome
entries: You know the {@spell Minor Illusion} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Speak with Animals} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.

items.name: Rock Gnome
entries: You know the {@spell Mending} and {@spell Prestidigitation} cantrips. In addition, you can spend 10 minutes casting {@spell Prestidigitation} to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP), such as a toy, fire starter, or music box. When you create the device, you determine its function by choosing one effect from {@spell Prestidigitation}; the device produces that effect whenever you or another creature takes a {@variantrule Bonus Action} to activate it with a touch. If the chosen effect has options within it, you choose one of those options for the device when you create it. For example, if you choose the spell's ignite-extinguish effect, you determine whether the device ignites or extinguishes fire; the device doesn't do both. You can have three such devices in existence at a time, and each falls apart 8 hours after its creation or when you dismantle it with a touch as a {@action Utilize} action.

items.name: Gnomish Lineage (Forest Gnome)
entries: You know the {@spell Minor Illusion} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Speak with Animals} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.

items.name: Gnomish Lineage (Rock Gnome)
entries: You know the {@spell Mending} and {@spell Prestidigitation} cantrips. In addition, you can spend 10 minutes casting {@spell Prestidigitation} to create a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 HP), such as a toy, fire starter, or music box. When you create the device, you determine its function by choosing one effect from {@spell Prestidigitation}; the device produces that effect whenever you or another creature takes a {@variantrule Bonus Action} to activate it with a touch. If the chosen effect has options within it, you choose one of those options for the device when you create it. For example, if you choose the spell's ignite-extinguish effect, you determine whether the device ignites or extinguishes fire; the device doesn't do both. You can have three such devices in existence at a time, and each falls apart 8 hours after its creation or when you dismantle it with a touch as a {@action Utilize} action.

entries.name: Age
entries: Goblins reach adulthood at age 8 and live up to 60 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Goblins are between 3 and 4 feet tall and weigh between 40 and 80 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fury of the Small
entries: When you damage a creature with an attack or a spell and the creature's size is larger than yours, you can cause the attack or spell to deal extra damage to the creature. The extra damage equals your level. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Nimble Escape
entries: You can take the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

entries.name: Age
entries: Goliaths have lifespans comparable to humans. They enter adulthood in their late teens and usually live less than a century.

entries.name: Size
entries: Goliaths are between 7 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 280 and 340 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Natural Athlete
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics} skill.

entries.name: Stone's Endurance
entries: You can focus yourself to occasionally shrug off injury. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a {@dice d12}. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Mountain Born
entries: You have resistance to cold damage. You're also acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Giant.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (about 2-3 feet tall)

entries.name: Brave
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened} condition.

entries.name: Halfling Nimbleness
entries: You can move through the space of any creature that is a size larger than you, but you can't stop in the same space.

entries.name: Luck
entries: When you roll a 1 on the {@dice d20} of a {@variantrule D20 Test}, you can reroll the die, and you must use the new roll.

entries.name: Naturally Stealthy
entries: You can take the {@action Hide} action even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

entries.name: Age
entries: Hobgoblins mature at the same rate as humans and have lifespans similar in length to theirs.

entries.name: Size
entries: Hobgoblins are between 5 and 6 feet tall and weigh between 150 and 200 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Martial Training
entries: You are proficient with two {@filter martial weapons|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=martial weapon} of your choice and with light armor.

entries.name: Saving Face
entries: Hobgoblins are careful not to show weakness in front of their allies, for fear of losing status. If you miss with an attack roll or fail an ability check or a saving throw, you can gain a bonus to the roll equal to the number of allies you can see within 30 feet of you (maximum bonus of +5). Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall) or Small (about 2-4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

entries.name: Resourceful
entries: You gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration} whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}.

entries.name: Skillful
entries: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

entries.name: Versatile
entries: You gain an {@filter Origin feat|feats|category=o} of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Lizardfolk reach maturity around age 14 and rarely live longer than 60 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Lizardfolk are a little bulkier and taller than humans, and their colorful frills make them appear even larger. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Swim Speed
entries: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Bite
entries: Your fanged maw is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with it, you deal piercing damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

entries.name: Cunning Artisan
entries: As part of a short rest, you can harvest bone and hide from a slain beast, construct, dragon, monstrosity, or plant creature of size small or larger to create one of the following items: a {@item shield|phb}, a {@item club|phb}, a {@item javelin|phb}, or {@dice 1d4} {@item dart|phb|darts} or {@item blowgun needle|phb|blowgun needles}. To use this trait, you need a blade, such as a {@item dagger|phb}, or appropriate {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools}, such as {@item leatherworker's tools|phb}.

entries.name: Hold Breath
entries: You can hold your breath for up to 15 minutes at a time.

entries.name: Hunter's Lore
entries: You gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, and {@skill Survival}.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: You have tough, scaly skin. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 13 + your Dexterity modifier. You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

entries.name: Hungry Jaws
entries: In battle, you can throw yourself into a vicious feeding frenzy. As a bonus action, you can make a special attack with your bite. If the attack hits, it deals its normal damage, and you gain temporary hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1), and you can't use this trait again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

entries.name: Age
entries: Orcs reach adulthood at age 12 and live up to 50 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Orcs are usually over 6 feet tall and weigh between 230 and 280 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Aggressive
entries: As a bonus action, you can move up to your movement speed toward a hostile creature you can see or hear. You must end this move closer to the enemy than you started.

entries.name: Primal Intuition
entries: You have proficiency in two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Medicine}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Perception}, and {@skill Survival}.

entries.name: Powerful Build
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Orc.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tabaxi have lifespans equivalent to humans.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tabaxi are taller on average than humans and relatively slender. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have a cat's keen senses, especially in the dark. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Feline Agility
entries: Your reflexes and agility allow you to move with a burst of speed. When you move on your turn in combat, you can double your speed until the end of the turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.

entries.name: Cat's Claws
entries: Because of your claws, you have a climbing speed of 20 feet. In addition, your claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to {@damage 1d4} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

entries.name: Cat's Talents
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} and {@skill Stealth} skills.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall) or Small (about 3-4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Fiendish Legacy
entries: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a legacy from the Fiendish Legacies table. You gain the level 1 benefit of the chosen legacy.

When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the legacy).

entries.caption: Fiendish Legacies
rows: You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Poison damage. You also know the {@spell Poison Spray} cantrip.
You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Necrotic damage. You also know the {@spell Chill Touch} cantrip.
You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Fire damage. You also know the {@spell Fire Bolt} cantrip.

entries.name: Otherworldly Presence
entries: You know the {@spell Thaumaturgy} cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you use for your Fiendish Legacy Trait.

items.name: Fiendish Legacy (Abyssal)
entries: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Poison damage. You also know the {@spell Poison Spray} cantrip.

When you reach character level 3, you learn the {@spell Ray of Sickness} spell. When you reach character level 5, you also learn the {@spell Hold Person} spell. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each spell once without a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the legacy).

items.name: Fiendish Legacy (Chthonic)
entries: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Necrotic damage. You also know the {@spell Chill Touch} cantrip.

When you reach character level 3, you learn the {@spell False Life} spell. When you reach character level 5, you also learn the {@spell Ray of Enfeeblement} spell. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each spell once without a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the legacy).

items.name: Fiendish Legacy (Infernal)
entries: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. You have {@variantrule Resistance} to Fire damage. You also know the {@spell Fire Bolt} cantrip.

When you reach character level 3, you learn the {@spell Hellish Rebuke} spell. When you reach character level 5, you also learn the {@spell Darkness} spell. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each spell once without a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the legacy).

entries.name: Age
entries: Tritons reach maturity around age 15 and can live up to 200 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tritons are slightly shorter than humans, averaging about 5 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Swim Speed
entries: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Amphibious
entries: You can breathe air and water.

entries.name: Control Air and Water
entries: A child of the sea, you can call on the magic of elemental air and water. You can cast {@spell fog cloud} with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast {@spell gust of wind} with it, and starting at 5th level, you can also cast {@spell wall of water|xge} with it. Once you cast a spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Emissary of the Sea
entries: Aquatic beasts have an extraordinary affinity with your people. You can communicate simple ideas with beasts that can breathe water. They can understand the meaning of your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return.

entries.name: Guardians of the Depths
entries: Adapted to even the most extreme ocean depths, you have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Primordial.
